lede: A look at February 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kurt Cobain and Gordon Brown, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Nagorno-Karabakh Autonomous Oblast
events.desc: The Nagorno-Karabakh Autonomous Oblast votes to secede from Azerbaijan and join Armenia, triggering the First Nagorno-Karabakh War.

events.title: Ranger 8
events.desc: Ranger 8 crashes into the Moon after a successful mission of photographing possible landing sites for the Apollo program astronauts.

events.title: Project Mercury
events.desc: Mercury program: While aboard Friendship 7, John Glenn becomes the first American to orbit the Earth, making three orbits in four hours, 55 minutes.

events.title: The Saturday Evening Post
events.desc: The Saturday Evening Post publishes the first of Norman Rockwell's Four Freedoms in support of United States President Franklin Roosevelt's 1941 State of the Union address theme of Four Freedoms.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Lieutenant Edward O'Hare becomes America's first World War II flying ace.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler secretly meets with German industrialists to arrange for financing of the Nazi Party's upcoming election campaign.

events.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art
events.desc: The Metropolitan Museum of Art opens in New York City.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Olustee: The largest battle fought in Florida during the war.

events.title: 1835 Concepción earthquake
events.desc: The 1835 Concepción earthquake destroys Concepción, Chile.

events.title: Milan
events.desc: The Milanese army and the St. George's (San Giorgio) Mercenaries of Lodrisio Visconti clash in the Battle of Parabiago; Visconti is defeated.

births.name: Gordon Brown
births.line: Scottish politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: Antonio Inoki
births.line: Japanese wrestler, mixed martial artist, and politician

births.name: Robert Huber
births.line: German biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Maria Goeppert Mayer, German-American physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Francisco Marto and Jacinta Marto
